commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the Nextcloud Talk bot-preflight non-OK `response.text()` read with an 8 KiB `readResponseTextLimited` read and adds a mocked regression test for oversized error bodies.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +46. Total +53 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and `v2026.6.10` call `response.text()` on a non-OK bot-admin preflight response; a mocked oversized streaming Response can exercise the path without real credentials.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No repair lane is needed because there are no actionable code findings; the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus normal exact-head validation.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): bound bot preflight error reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Nextcloud Talk bot response preflight could read unbounded HTTP error bodies via `response.text()` when the bot-admin API returns a failure status, risking gateway memory pressure during channel startup checks.

## Why This Change Was Made

The preflight probe already bounds successful JSON reads via `readProviderJsonResponse`, but the HTTP error path still called `response.text()` directly. This aligns error handling with `send.ts` by using `readResponseTextLimited` with an 8 KiB cap before building the diagnostic message.

## User Impact

Operators still get the same bot preflight failure diagnostics when the Nextcloud API errors, without loading arbitrarily large error payloads into memory.

## Evidence

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/nextcloud-talk/src/bot-preflight.test.ts
```

Added a regression test that returns an oversized error body through a tracked stream, asserts `response.text()` is never called, and verifies the stream is canceled after the bounded read.

AI-assisted: implementation and tests were drafted with Cursor agent assistance; validation was run locally with the command above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
